CDISC Definition: Terminology for the test codes concerned with the increase in overall body mass.
NCI Preferred Term: CDISC SEND Body Weight Gain Test Code Terminology

CDISC Definition: A change in overall body weight, relative to the beginning of the collection period.
The mean value of the amount of weight change over a period of time, relative to the beginning of the collection period.

CDISC Synonym(s): Gravid Uterus Adjusted Body Weight Gain
CDISC Definition: The amount of maternal body weight gained over a period of time where the weight at the end of the interval is adjusted for the gravid uterus weight. This is derived by subtracting the maternal body weight adjusted for the gravid uterus from the total maternal body weight.
NCI Preferred Term: Gravid Uterus Adjusted Maternal Body Weight Gain

CDISC Definition: The amount of weight change over a period of time in relation to the total weight, relative to the beginning of the collection period.
